_meta.notes: Devices marked suite_only are unavailable in Standard/Intro editions. iron_static_potential rates usefulness for IRON STATIC aesthetics: high/medium/low.

instruments.name: Analog
instruments.description: 2-oscillator analog-modeled subtractive synth with 2 filters, 2 envelopes, 2 LFOs, and MPE support. Deep and fat.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Direct NIN-style analog bass and pad workhorse. Two filters in series can do brutal low-pass → HP cascades. Use when hardware Rev2/Take5 unavailable.

instruments.name: Collision
instruments.description: Mallet percussion physical modeler (Applied Acoustics). Mallet or Noise exciter feeding 2 resonators (beam, marimba, string, membrane, plate, pipe, tube).
instruments.iron_static_notes: Ideal IRON STATIC percussive texture generator. Membrane + Plate resonators at extreme inharm settings produce industrial clang and scrap-metal hits. Essential when DFAM is unavailable.

instruments.name: Drift
instruments.description: Lean subtractive synth with 2 oscillators, dynamic filter, dual envelopes (Env2 can cycle), LFO, and MPE. Low CPU. Fast to program.
key_parameters: LP filter freq, res, key tracking
instruments.iron_static_notes: Excellent quick-sketch synth when you need a fast patch without deep menu diving. Good MPE candidate for expressive lead lines.

instruments.name: Drum Sampler
instruments.description: One-shot sample player designed for Drum Racks. AHD envelope, filter, 9 playback effects (Stretch, Loop, Pitch Env, Punch, 8-Bit, FM, Ring Mod, Sub Osc, Noise).
instruments.iron_static_notes: Use inside Drum Racks for industrial sample hits. Sub Osc playback effect layers a sine sub under any sample — useful for thickening Digitakt-style kicks when playing from Ableton.

instruments.name: Electric
instruments.description: Electric piano physical modeler (Applied Acoustics). Hammer, fork, damper/pickup, and body modeled after classic '70s instruments.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Outside core IRON STATIC palette but can produce unusual metallic bell timbres when parameters are pushed beyond realistic ranges.

instruments.name: External Instrument
instruments.description: Sends MIDI out to hardware or multitimbral plugins, returns audio in. The correct way to integrate hardware synths into Live tracks.
instruments.iron_static_notes: This is how every IRON STATIC hardware synth lives inside Ableton. One External Instrument per hardware instrument track. MIDI To = the hardware's port, Audio From = its audio input.

instruments.name: Impulse
instruments.description: 8-slot drum sampler with per-slot start, transpose, stretch, filter, saturation, envelope, pan/volume. Velocity and random modulation on most params.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Good Digitakt surrogate when building beats entirely in the box. Per-slot saturation and filter give enough grit. Less expressive than DFAM for machine percussion but more controllable.

instruments.name: Meld
instruments.description: Dual macro oscillator engines, each with exotic waveform algorithms (Noisy Shapes, Harmonic FM, Fold FM, Squelch, Chip, Shepard's Pi, Chord, etc.), filter, envelopes, 2 LFOs, and MPE modulation matrix.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Closest built-in equivalent to Arturia Pigments' weird engine. Fold FM, Squelch, and Shepard's Pi oscillators produce sounds that feel genuinely wrong — perfect for industrial textures when Pigments is unavailable.

instruments.name: Operator
instruments.description: 4-operator FM synth with 11 algorithms, per-oscillator envelope/waveform/feedback, LP/BP filter, LFO, pitch envelope. Classic Yamaha DX-style FM plus extended waveforms and per-operator modulation.
key_parameters: Algorithm (11 types — controls operator carrier/modulator routing)
OSC A/B/C/D: waveform, freq ratio/fixed, level, feedback, envelope
LFO waveform, range, rate, dest A+B
instruments.iron_static_notes: Essential IRON STATIC voice. FM synthesis produces metallic attack transients, harsh bell tones, and industrial stabs that sit perfectly in heavy electronic music. The feedback oscillator alone can produce controlled noise. Think NIN 'Closer' bell hits.

instruments.name: Sampler
instruments.description: Full-featured multi-sample instrument with zone editor, loop controls, pitch/filter/amplitude envelopes, LFO, extensive MIDI modulation matrix, and multi-layer support.
key_parameters: MIDI tab: Vel/Key/AT/MW/PB to 2 destinations each
instruments.iron_static_notes: Use for complex sample-based instruments. Load industrial field recordings, steel hits, or noise textures as multisampled instruments. More powerful than Simpler for anything requiring velocity layers.

instruments.name: Simpler
instruments.description: Single-sample player with 3 modes: Classic (looping, ADSR, polyphonic), One-Shot (monophonic, simplified), and Slicing (chop to notes). Supports Live's warping engine.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Fastest way to get a sample making notes. Drag a Digitakt recording or field recording in and immediately sequence it. Slicing mode can chop a noise bed into playable glitch fragments.

instruments.name: Tension
instruments.description: String physical modeler (Applied Acoustics). Exciter (bow/hammer/plectrum/blow), string resonator, damper, termination, pickup, body. Full filter section with LFO and envelopes.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Bow exciter at high friction with extreme decay produces sustained metallic scrape — useful for industrial texture beds. Push parameters beyond physical realism for sounds no real string could make.

instruments.name: Wavetable
instruments.description: 2-oscillator wavetable synth with per-oscillator wavetable selection, position sweep, oscillator effects (Classic/Modern/FM/AM), 2 filter slots (4 circuit types: OB-6/MG/MS2/SMP/PRD-modeled), extensive modulation matrix, unison, and MPE.
instruments.iron_static_notes: Industrial pad and texture machine. Wavetable sweep through noise/digital/metallic tables combined with PRD (Moog-modeled) filter produces exactly the kind of evolving industrial bed IRON STATIC needs. Strong Pigments substitute.

audio_effects.name: Amp
audio_effects.description: 7 guitar amp models (Softube physical modeling). Clean, Boost, Blues, Rock, Lead, Heavy, Bass.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Lead and Heavy models are the obvious IRON STATIC tools. Run synths through them — especially the Minibrute 2S — for instant amp-driven distortion. Stack with Cabinet for full amp sim chain.

audio_effects.name: Auto Filter
audio_effects.description: Resonant filter with LFO, envelope follower, and sidechain. Multiple filter types including classic analog-modeled circuits.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Envelope-follower mode keys filter sweeps to incoming audio dynamics — essential for sidechain-reactive filter effects. PRD circuit (Moog-modeled ladder) is the warmest.

audio_effects.name: Auto Pan-Tremolo
audio_effects.description: LFO-driven panning and tremolo effect with sync and phase controls.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Good for adding rhythmic movement to pads or drones. Sync to song tempo for locked tremolo effects.

audio_effects.name: Auto Shift
audio_effects.description: Real-time pitch tracking and correction with vibrato, pitch shifting, formant shifting, LFO, and MIDI sidechain input for harmonization.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: MIDI mode enables real-time vocal harmonization — send MIDI notes from Digitakt to control harmony intervals. Also useful for pitch-correcting rough synth tuning in live settings.

audio_effects.name: Beat Repeat
audio_effects.description: Probabilistic beat repeater and slicer. Captures audio and repeats, stutters, or slices it based on interval and chance settings.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Signature IRON STATIC glitch tool. On drum buses or synth returns, Beat Repeat introduces the kind of controlled chaos that makes machine music feel alive. Set low Chance and let it fire unpredictably.

audio_effects.name: Cabinet
audio_effects.description: 5 guitar cabinet simulations (Softube) with mic selection (On-Axis/Off-Axis/Far) and Dynamic/Condenser mic choice.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Use after Amp to complete the amp sim chain. 4x12 cab with on-axis dynamic mic for heaviest color.

audio_effects.name: Channel EQ
audio_effects.description: 3-band EQ inspired by classic mixing desk channels. HP 80Hz switch, 100Hz low shelf, sweepable mid peak (120Hz–7.5kHz), high shelf.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Fast channel strip EQ. Good default on every track for quick tone shaping without overthinking it.

audio_effects.name: Chorus-Ensemble
audio_effects.description: Chorus and ensemble effect with Classic and Ensemble modes.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Useful for widening Rev2-style pads when the hardware isn't available, but rarely the right tool for the IRON STATIC aesthetic which prefers grit over shimmer.

audio_effects.name: Compressor
audio_effects.description: Full-featured compressor with sidechain, peak/RMS detection, and soft knee. Also supports expansion/gating.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Standard tracking compressor. Use on drums and bass. Heavy attack (fast) + short release for transient punch.

audio_effects.name: Corpus
audio_effects.description: Audio-rate resonator modeling 7 physical objects (beam, marimba, string, membrane, plate, pipe, tube). MIDI sidechain for pitch control. Applied Acoustics.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Run noise, synth stabs, or Digitakt hits through Corpus with MIDI sidechain from the same sequence. The audio gets pitched and resonated to match your notes — instant tuned industrial percussion from any signal.

audio_effects.name: Delay
audio_effects.description: Simple stereo delay with sync, feedback, and filter.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Go-to simple delay. Use Echo instead for tape character or complex modulation.

audio_effects.name: Drum Buss
audio_effects.description: Analog-style drum bus processor: compressor, 3-type distortion (Soft/Medium/Hard), transient shaper (Trim, Attack, Sustain), boom (sub resonator), Dry/Wet.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Essential for IRON STATIC drum groups. Boom adds low-end weight to Digitakt loops, Hard distortion adds grit. Transient Attack tightens flabby drum hits.

audio_effects.name: Dynamic Tube
audio_effects.description: Tube saturation with 3 models (A/B/C) and an envelope follower that dynamically modulates the bias point. Makes distortion respond to signal dynamics.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Envelope-reactive tube saturation that gets dirtier as the signal gets louder — this is exactly how real tube amps behave. Use on synth buses where you want saturation that breathes with the dynamics.

audio_effects.name: Echo
audio_effects.description: Dual stereo delay with tape/analog character, filter, modulation (chorus, vibrato, noise, wobble), stereo/ping-pong modes, and a gate/ducking output.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: The default delay for IRON STATIC. Tape wobble + filter + feedback creates the kind of degraded, industrial echo that suits the aesthetic. Use driven input for grit inside the delay trail.

audio_effects.name: EQ Eight
audio_effects.description: 8-band parametric EQ. Stereo, L/R, and M/S modes. 8 filter types per band (low cut, low shelf, bell, high shelf, high cut, notch, etc.).
key_parameters: Up to 8 bands, each: type, freq, gain, Q
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Standard surgical EQ for mixing. Use M/S mode to tighten the center or widen the sides of a synth layer.

audio_effects.name: EQ Three
audio_effects.description: DJ-style 3-band EQ (Low/Mid/High) with kill switches. -inf to +6 dB per band.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Live DJ EQ for performance use. Assign Low to a macro or MIDI key for instant bass kill.

audio_effects.name: Erosion
audio_effects.description: Digital degradation via modulated short delay with noise or sine. Creates aliasing, downsampling artifacts, and noisy digital distortion.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Pure IRON STATIC texture tool. Wide Noise mode with moderate frequency adds a layer of digital grit that sounds like bit rot or signal interference. Subtle amounts are more interesting than maxed settings.

audio_effects.name: External Audio Effect
audio_effects.description: Routes audio to hardware effects processors and returns it. Handles latency compensation manually.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Use this to patch Ableton audio through hardware effects boxes or guitar pedals and return it into the session.

audio_effects.name: Filter Delay
audio_effects.description: 3 independent delay lines with individual HP/LP filters before each delay. Left channel, L+R sum, Right channel.
key_parameters: Per delay: on/off, HP/LP filter, delay time (ms or sync), feedback, pan, volume
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Good for creating rhythmically separated echoes that sit in different frequency ranges — e.g., the high-frequency delay faster than the bass delay.

audio_effects.name: Gate
audio_effects.description: Noise gate with sidechain input, envelope controls, and lookahead.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Sidechain the gate to the kick drum for rhythmically gated pads — a classic heavy electronic technique.

audio_effects.name: Glue Compressor
audio_effects.description: Bus compressor modeled on classic SSL-style bus compressors. Fast attack options, auto-release, sidechain.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Default mix bus compressor. 4:1 ratio, auto release, modest threshold for mix glue without killing dynamics.

audio_effects.name: Grain Delay
audio_effects.description: Granular delay — slices audio into grains and delays/pitch-shifts them independently. Extreme randomization creates complex textural masses.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Extreme Grain Delay settings turn any input into a smear of noise — useful for transition textures or making a clean synth sound like it's disintegrating. Keep feedback below 80% to avoid runaway.

audio_effects.name: Hybrid Reverb
audio_effects.description: Combined convolution (IR) + algorithmic reverb. 5 algorithmic modes: Dark Hall, Quartz, Shimmer, Tides, Prism. Freeze mode.
key_parameters: Algorithm-specific params (Damping, Lo Damp, Mod, Shimmer pitch, etc.)
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: IRON STATIC default reverb. Shimmer mode adds pitch-shifted feedback reverb — good for dramatic swells. Tides and Dark Hall for dark industrial spaces. Use with Freeze for infinite sustain pads.

audio_effects.name: Limiter
audio_effects.description: Brickwall limiter with lookahead for master bus and loudness control.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Master bus only. Don't use as a creative tool.

audio_effects.name: Looper
audio_effects.description: Real-time audio looper. Record, overdub, play, reverse, speed. Sync to Live transport or free-running.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Live loop building tool. Less useful in the IRON STATIC clip-based workflow but good for improvised texture layers in live performance.

audio_effects.name: Multiband Dynamics
audio_effects.description: 3-band compressor/expander with per-band threshold, ratio, attack, release, and gain. Can compress and expand simultaneously.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Use on the master or drum bus for dynamic control that doesn't squash everything equally. Expand low mids for pumping industrial impact.

audio_effects.name: Overdrive
audio_effects.description: Guitar pedal-inspired overdrive with bandpass filter pre-distortion, drive, tone, and dynamics (compression/expansion control).
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Better on synths than a typical distortion — the pre-drive bandpass filter lets you focus exactly which frequency range gets destroyed. Set narrow band on upper mids for nasal, biting distortion.

audio_effects.name: Pedal
audio_effects.description: 3 guitar pedal types: Overdrive (warm/smooth), Distortion (tight/aggressive), Fuzz (unstable/broken). 3-band EQ. Sub bass extension switch.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Fuzz with Sub enabled is the heaviest thing in Live's built-in toolkit. Run Rev2 bass patches or Operator through Fuzz + Sub for massive low-end distortion when Minibrute 2S Brute Factor isn't available.

audio_effects.name: Phaser-Flanger
audio_effects.description: Combined phaser and flanger with a continuous morph between modes. LFO, envelope follower, and feedback controls.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Subtle phaser on pads adds movement without being obviously 'swirly'. Flanging on noise or distorted synths creates metallic sweep effects.

audio_effects.name: Redux
audio_effects.description: Bitcrusher and sample rate reducer. Downsampling (with vintage mode) and bit reduction (with dithering) sections, each independently controllable.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Essential digital destruction tool. Low sample rate (8kHz range) + low bit depth (8-bit) produces classic lo-fi crunch. Even subtle Redux adds digital texture without obvious lo-fi coloring.

audio_effects.name: Resonators
audio_effects.description: 5 parallel resonators tunable in semitones or scale degrees. Takes any audio input and adds pitched, sustained resonance.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Detune resonators to cluster intervals (minor 2nd, tritone) for dissonant resonant beds from any input signal. Use on noise or room recordings to give them pitch and tonal character.

audio_effects.name: Reverb
audio_effects.description: Classic algorithmic reverb. Pre-delay, diffusion network, high/low cut, stereo, density controls.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Use Hybrid Reverb for more character. Classic Reverb is useful for transparent, clean reverb when you don't want IR coloring.

audio_effects.name: Roar
audio_effects.description: Multi-stage saturation device (up to 3 stages) with 12 shaper curves, 9 filter types per stage, flexible routing (serial/parallel/mid-side), feedback with tunable pitch, built-in compressor, envelope follower, LFO, modulation matrix, and sidechain.
key_parameters: Per stage: Shaper type (12 curves), Amount, Bias, Filter type (9 types), Frequency
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: The most powerful built-in saturation device. Three-stage M-S mode can saturate just the mid channel while leaving the sides clean. Feedback with Note mode pitches the feedback resonance to the incoming MIDI note — creates a tuned feedback instrument out of any audio.

audio_effects.name: Saturator
audio_effects.description: Waveshaping saturator with 8 curve types (Analog Clip, Soft Sine, Bass Shaper, Medium Curve, Hard Curve, Sinoid Fold, Digital Clip, Waveshaper). Pre/post EQ.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Sinoid Fold is the most interesting curve for electronic music — produces wavefolding harmonics similar to West Coast synthesis. Use on Subharmonicon outputs for added harmonic content.

audio_effects.name: Shifter
audio_effects.description: Multi-purpose pitch and frequency shifter. Pitch shifting, ring modulation, frequency shifting (inharmonic). LFO, envelope follower, MIDI sidechain for note-controlled shifting.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Frequency Shift mode (not pitch shift) produces inharmonic, robotic, ring-mod-like timbres. Run drone pads through frequency shift for the metallic robotic coloring that defines a lot of industrial electronic music.

audio_effects.name: Spectral Blur
audio_effects.description: Spectral blurring effect that smears audio across time in the frequency domain. Creates washy, diffuse, ambient textures.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Good for creating reverb-like smear from short stabs or transients. Run percussive elements through Spectral Blur to create textural ghost tails.

audio_effects.name: Spectral Resonator
audio_effects.description: Spectral resonator that applies tuned resonances to audio's frequency spectrum via MIDI input (up to 16-voice polyphony). Modulation modes: Wander, Granular, and others.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Play chords on the keyboard while feeding noise or a drum loop through Spectral Resonator — the drums become harmonically tuned to your chord. Vocoder-like but more spectral and weirder. Use with Digitakt providing the MIDI input.

audio_effects.name: Spectral Time
audio_effects.description: Spectral delay and freezing effect that processes audio in the frequency domain. Per-frequency delay and diffusion.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Freeze mode captures a spectral snapshot and holds it indefinitely — creates sustained tonal drones from transient hits. Feed a single Digitakt snare hit into Spectral Time with Freeze for an infinite smeared pad.

audio_effects.name: Spectrum
audio_effects.description: Real-time spectrum analyzer. No audio processing.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Analysis only. Drop it on a channel to see what's happening spectrally.

audio_effects.name: Tuner
audio_effects.description: Chromatic tuner for audio input. Classic (needle) and Histogram views. Reference adjustable 410–480 Hz.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Use on hardware synth tracks to tune them before recording.

audio_effects.name: Utility
audio_effects.description: Gain, stereo width, mono, bass mono, mute, and phase invert. Essential signal utility.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Always available, always useful. Bass Mono collapses low frequencies to mono for cleaner low-end in the mix.

audio_effects.name: Vinyl Distortion
audio_effects.description: Vinyl record simulation: even harmonics (Tracing Model), odd harmonics (Pinch Effect), crackle generator. Soft (dub plate) or Hard mode.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Crackle + Pinch in Hard mode on synth pads adds aged vinyl texture. Good for lo-fi character without obvious bitcrushing.

audio_effects.name: Vocoder
audio_effects.description: Classic vocoder with external carrier input or internal noise/external synthesis carriers. Adjustable band count and formant shift.
audio_effects.iron_static_notes: Run vocals or speech through Vocoder with Rev2 or Wavetable as carrier for classic robotic vocal treatment. IRON STATIC occasionally uses vocoder for spoken word industrial textures.

midi_effects.name: Arpeggiator
midi_effects.description: Feature-complete arpeggiator with 15+ patterns, sync, gate, transposing over multiple octaves, groove, velocity decay, hold, and retrigger.
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: On hardware synths via External Instrument, Arpeggiator turns a single held chord into a pattern without programming MIDI notes. Combine with Digitakt clock for synced sequenced arpeggio that's separate from the Digitakt's own sequences.

midi_effects.name: CC Control
midi_effects.description: Sends MIDI CC messages. 3 fixed controls (Mod Wheel, Pitch Bend, Pressure) + 1 custom toggle + 12 custom knobs. Each assignable to any CC number.
key_parameters: Mod Wheel, Pitch Bend, Pressure (fixed CCs)
Custom A (on/off CC, default CC64 sustain)
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Essential for controlling hardware synth parameters from Ableton automation lanes. Map Custom B-M to Rev2 NRPNs or Take5 CC parameters and automate them in the arrangement.

midi_effects.name: Chord
midi_effects.description: Generates up to 6 additional notes per input note, each with independent pitch offset (±36 semitones), velocity %, chance %, and strum timing.
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Program chord voicings as MIDI effects on a hardware synth track. Single mono sequence plays as polyphonic chords on the Rev2 or Take5. Use minor 2nd + tritone + minor 7th for IRON STATIC dissonance.

midi_effects.name: Note Length
midi_effects.description: Alters MIDI note lengths. Can trigger notes from Note Off instead of Note On. Gate control, Key Scale (pitch-dependent length).
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Note Off trigger mode creates unusual rhythmic feels where the timing of release determines the pattern rather than attack. Good for percussive performance experimentation.

midi_effects.name: Pitch
midi_effects.description: Transposes MIDI notes ±128 semitones. Scale-degree mode available. Range/Lowest to filter allowed notes. Block/Fold/Limit modes for out-of-range notes.
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Quick transposition for live use. Map Step Up/Down to hardware buttons for on-the-fly octave or scale-step transposition.

midi_effects.name: Random
midi_effects.description: Randomizes pitch of incoming MIDI notes by chance, within a range defined by Choices and Interval. Random or Alt (round-robin) mode.
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Alt mode + Bi sign creates a deterministic ping-pong pitch sequence from a single held note — instant melodic variation without programming. Use on Take5 for unpredictable lead lines.

midi_effects.name: Scale
midi_effects.description: Remaps MIDI notes to a defined scale via a 13x13 note matrix. User-defined scales supported. Transpose by semitones.
key_parameters: Lowest + Range (limit remapping to note range)
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Force all MIDI input from any source — Push, external keyboard, Arpeggiator — into A Phrygian or whatever the active song key is. Drop Scale before any hardware synth channel and the whole session is locked to key.

midi_effects.name: Velocity
midi_effects.description: Remaps incoming MIDI velocities. Gate, clip, and fixed modes. Random modulation. Drive and Compand controls for non-linear velocity curves.
key_parameters: Operation (Note On velocity / Note Off / Both)
midi_effects.iron_static_notes: Fixed mode sets all velocities to Out Hi — useful for leveling performance inconsistencies from a hardware sequencer. Random adds humanization to machine-quantized sequences.
